concept: Breeze / Gentle Wind / Soft Air Movement
description: A light, gentle wind that refreshes without force. 'Sihewa' names air's gentlest touch—the cooling movement on a warm day, the whisper through leaves, wind at its most welcoming and kind.
sound_symbolism: The word sighs gently. 'Si' begins with soft sibilance, air barely moving. Then 'he' breathes with aspirate lightness, the gentlest push, while 'wa' glides open and releases the breeze onward.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'sihewa' (breeze), 'lo sihewa' (breezes). A breeze is not a detached or crafted object; a counted episode may take optional event classifier 'nophe', or the classifier may be omitted. Distinguished from 'howeli' (wind) by gentleness—breeze refreshes, wind moves.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Breezes teach subtle awareness—the lightest touch still reaches us when we are present enough to notice.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The breeze is nature's gentlest touch—invisible yet felt, animating leaves and hair with effortless grace.
